expected_main_papers.title: Distilling the Knowledge in a Neural Network
expected_main_papers.note: Introduces knowledge distillation as a model compression method.

expected_main_papers.title: DistilBERT, a distilled version of BERT: smaller, faster, cheaper and lighter
expected_main_papers.note: Applies distillation to transformer language models for cheaper inference.

expected_main_papers.title: LLM.int8(): 8-bit Matrix Multiplication for Transformers at Scale
expected_main_papers.note: Introduces an 8-bit inference method handling outlier features in large transformers.

expected_main_papers.title: SmoothQuant: Accurate and Efficient Post-Training Quantization for Large Language Models
expected_main_papers.note: Smooths activation outliers to enable efficient weight-activation quantization.

expected_main_papers.title: GPTQ: Accurate Post-Training Quantization for Generative Pre-trained Transformers
expected_main_papers.note: Uses second-order information for accurate post-training quantization.

expected_main_papers.title: To Prune, or Not to Prune: Exploring the Efficacy of Pruning for Model Compression
expected_main_papers.note: Studies pruning as a practical compression method.

expected_main_papers.title: A Review of Sparse Expert Models in Deep Learning
expected_main_papers.note: Surveys sparse expert models and the MoE design space.

expected_main_papers.title: Efficient Transformers: A Survey
expected_main_papers.note: Surveys architectural approaches to efficient transformer computation.

expected_concept_sections.name: 1. The inference bottleneck
required_ideas: large Transformer inference is limited by memory, bandwidth, attention cost, and latency
distinguish parameter storage, activation movement, and decoding throughput
motivate compression and architectural optimization

expected_concept_sections.name: 2. Distillation
required_ideas: student models imitate teacher outputs or representations
distillation reduces inference cost
include both general distillation and DistilBERT-style Transformer distillation

expected_concept_sections.name: 3. Quantization
required_ideas: low-bit weights/activations reduce memory and bandwidth
outlier handling in LLM.int8
SmoothQuant activation smoothing and GPTQ post-training quantization

expected_concept_sections.name: 4. Structured sparsity and pruning
required_ideas: pruning removes less important weights or structures
structured sparsity is more hardware-friendly
compression tradeoffs between accuracy and speed

expected_concept_sections.name: 5. Mixture-of-Experts
required_ideas: sparse expert routing activates only part of model capacity
MoE trades parameters for conditional compute
deployment challenges include routing, load balance, and memory

expected_concept_sections.name: 6. Architectural optimization
required_ideas: efficient attention and memory-saving transformer variants
recurrence, sparsity, or low-rank attention patterns
architecture-level changes reduce asymptotic or practical cost

expected_concept_sections.name: 7. Practical synthesis
required_ideas: real deployments combine quantization, kernels, batching, distillation, pruning, and architecture choices
optimization depends on latency, throughput, quality, hardware, and serving regime
avoid treating any single method as universally best

expected_figures.source: Fedus et al. 2022 / sparse expert models
Tay et al. 2022 / efficient Transformer survey

quality_checks: HTML opens in a local HTTP server with visible styling from stylesheet.min.css
LaTeX formulas render through MathJax rather than appearing as raw TeX when formulas are present
Blog is image-rich and every included figure has a visible source-paper attribution
The blog explains paper selection and section-paper mapping in selection_note.md
The final result directory includes the HTML and all referenced assets needed for offline viewing
The blog should be a coherent paper-reading article, not just a bullet list or copied abstracts
All HTML-referenced assets resolve relative to the delivered HTML path and return 200 under a local HTTP server
